name: semchunk
tagline: Split text into semantically meaningful chunks for embedding and retrieval
seoTitle: semchunk - Semantic Text Chunking for RAG
metaDescription: semchunk is a Python library that splits text into semantically meaningful chunks for RAG, working with any tokenizer including Tiktoken and Transformers.
overview: semchunk is a Python library for splitting text into smaller chunks while keeping as much local semantic context as possible. It uses a hierarchical chunking algorithm and works with any tokenizer or token counter, including those from Tiktoken and Transformers.

It is aimed at developers building retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines who need to break long documents into chunks before embedding and indexing them. You pass a tokenizer name or a custom token-counting function plus a target chunk size, and semchunk returns the chunks.

Within the parsing and ingestion stage of a RAG system, semchunk handles the splitting step. It supports chunk overlap, chunk offsets, multiprocessing for large batches, and an optional AI-powered chunking mode that uses Isaacus enrichment models.
features: Works with any tokenizer or token counter, including Tiktoken encodings, Transformers tokenizers, OpenAI model names, or a custom function
Returns chunk offsets when you pass offsets=True, so you can map chunks back to the source text
Supports chunk overlap by a ratio (values < 1) or an absolute token count (values >= 1)
Optional multiprocessing via the processes argument to speed up chunking large batches of texts
Optional AI-powered chunking using Isaacus enrichment models such as kanon-2-enricher
Available on PyPI and conda-forge, with a community Rust port (semchunk-rs)

gettingStarted.intro: Install semchunk from PyPI, then build a chunker by passing a tokenizer and a target chunk size.

steps.heading: Install semchunk
steps.body: Install with pip (or uv). It is also available on conda-forge.

steps.heading: Build a chunker and chunk text
steps.body: Create a chunker with chunkerify() by passing a tokenizer name (here an OpenAI Tiktoken encoding) and a chunk size, then call it on your text. Passing a list of texts returns a list of lists of chunks.
steps.code:
import semchunk

chunk_size = 4  # tokens per chunk
text = 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.'

chunker = semchunk.chunkerify('cl100k_base', chunk_size)

assert chunker(text) == ['The quick brown fox', 'jumps over the', 'lazy dog.']

steps.heading: Get offsets and overlap (optional)
steps.body: Pass offsets=True to also return where each chunk starts and ends, and overlap to overlap chunks by a ratio (< 1) or an absolute number of tokens (>= 1).

useCases: Splitting long documents into chunks before embedding them for a RAG pipeline
Preparing text for a vector database while keeping local semantic context intact
Chunking with the same tokenizer your embedding or LLM uses, so chunk sizes match the model's token budget
Processing large batches of documents quickly using multiprocessing
